expectations.text: A test for is_empty on a new stack appears before any Stack implementation
expectations.evidence: Cycle 1 RED phase shows test_new_stack_is_empty written first, producing 'ModuleNotFoundError: No module named stack' — confirming no Stack implementation existed yet. The minimal stub (returning constant True) was only added after the failing test was confirmed.

expectations.text: Tests cover all four methods: push, pop, peek, is_empty
expectations.evidence: test_stack.py contains tests exercising all four methods: is_empty (test_new_stack_is_empty, test_is_not_empty_after_push), push (test_is_not_empty_after_push, test_pop_returns_pushed_value, test_lifo_order), pop (test_pop_returns_pushed_value, test_pop_empty_stack_raises, test_lifo_order), peek (test_peek_returns_top_without_removing, test_peek_does_not_remove_element).

expectations.text: A test verifies that pop on an empty stack raises IndexError
expectations.evidence: test_pop_empty_stack_raises uses 'with pytest.raises(IndexError): s.pop()' on a fresh empty Stack. Confirmed passing in final test run.

expectations.text: All tests pass with the final implementation
expectations.evidence: Running 'python -m pytest test_stack.py -v' collected 7 items and reported '7 passed in 0.02s' with exit code 0.

expectations.text: Tests were added one at a time — no large batches of tests written before the code caught up
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows 7 strictly sequential cycles, each adding exactly one test (RED), then updating only enough code to pass it (GREEN). After each new test, the previous passing count is cited (e.g. '1 failed, 2 passed'), confirming one-test-at-a-time discipline throughout.

suggestions: Cycle 4 (pop on empty stack) and Cycles 6-7 passed immediately without a RED phase; the transcript acknowledged this honestly. A stricter TDD grader might flag the absence of a genuine RED, but the tests still add regression value and the process was otherwise disciplined.
Consider testing peek on an empty stack raises an appropriate error (IndexError/similar) to fully harden the contract.
eval_feedback.overall: Excellent TDD execution: the test list was planned upfront, each cycle added exactly one test before any implementation, all four required methods are covered, the IndexError case is verified, and all 7 tests pass. Minor note: three cycles never hit RED because the existing implementation already satisfied the new test, but this was transparently documented.
